videos.title: Wayve Secures $1.5B to deploy its global autonomy platform
videos.description:
This milestone reflects a broader shift toward end-to-end AI as a scalable path to autonomy, aligning leading technology companies, global automakers and mobility partners behind a unified autonomy platform.

In 2017, we pioneered AV2.0 when it was widely dismissed. Today, we’ve demonstrated zero-shot autonomy across 500+ cities using a single foundation model, showing autonomy can work everywhere, in every condition, across every type of vehicle.

That’s what makes Wayve different.

Our Series D funding allows us to scale what already works, bringing embodied AI to personally owned vehicles and robotaxis, at the same time. That’s the future we’re building.

videos.title: Safety Intelligence @ Wayve
videos.description:
With great capability comes great responsibility; building trust in Assisted or Automated Driving starts with safety. 

Our model is trained to optimize for safe driving behaviors, designed to navigate both everyday road hazards but also rare, high-risk scenarios.

By anticipating and managing risk in real time, the Wayve AI Driver doesn’t just react to danger, it works proactively to avoid and prevent it.

Our intelligence is fully portable across hardware platforms, so the Wayve AI Driver can power any vehicle, making safe and scalable autonomy a reality.

videos.title: Nissan Wayve Signing Ceremony
videos.description: Nissan and Wayve Sign Definitive Agreements to Deliver Next-Generation Driver Assistance Technology

videos.title: AI-500 Roadshow: Chicago to New York
videos.description: As part of our US Roadshow, the Wayve AI Driver set out on an ambitious 800-mile journey from the Great Lakes to the Big Apple. This route took our technology through Chicago, Detroit, Pittsburgh, and New York, four cities that challenge every aspect of driving.

videos.title: AI-500 Roadshow: Tokyo to Mt. Fuji
videos.description: This video captures the final chapter of our Global Roadshow: Japan, where our single global model adapted to complex urban streets and mountain passes going from Tokyo to Mt. Fuji.

videos.title: Alex Kendall takes Ivan Espinosa, President & CEO Nissan Motor Co. for a demo ride in Yokohama
videos.description:
Join Alex Kendall, CEO & Co-founder of Wayve, and Ivan Espinosa, President & CEO of Nissan Motor Co., on the streets of Yokohama for an exclusive look at the future of intelligent driving.

In this ride-along, Alex demonstrates the integration between Wayve’s AI Driver and Nissans Pro-Pilot on a Nissan Ariya Vehicle Platform. 

a next-generation, end-to-end autonomous driving system that learns directly from real-world experience.

Together, they explore how Embodied AI is transforming the path to safe, scalable autonomy - and what this means for automakers, drivers, and the next era of mobility.

videos.title: Are you ready to shape the future of mobility with us?
videos.description:
At Wayve, we’re reimagining autonomy for a new era of mobility.

We always believed there was a smarter path to autonomy. But there was no blueprint, just belief, that end-to-end learning could redefine how AI driving models understand the world.

Today, that belief has grown into a global effort powered by brilliant, mission-driven people who challenge what’s possible every day.

🎥 Watch our new film, meet the people behind the movement, and discover why we’re only just getting started.

videos.title: The Tokyo Challenge: SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son Experiences Wayve’s Embodied AI in Action
videos.description: To mark Wayve’s expansion to Japan, SoftBank Group’s Chairman and CEO Masayoshi Son joined Wayve Co-founder and CEO Alex Kendall for “The Tokyo Challenge,” a drive through Tokyo that put the Wayve AI Driver to the test. Watch as Son experiences firsthand how Wayve’s AV2.0 embodied AI navigates one of the world’s busiest driving environments, showcasing the technology’s adaptability and readiness to scale globally.

videos.title: Ride the Wayve: Through a Typhoon in Tokyo
videos.description:
What happens when a typhoon brings Japan’s famous Shinkansen bullet trains to a halt? 
The Wayve AI Driver keeps going.

In this video, watch the Wayve AI Driver safely navigate through heavy rain and storm conditions in Tokyo. Our AI doesn’t just “follow the rules” — it adapts in real time, adjusting its driving style to handle extreme weather and keep moving safely.

This is what the future of autonomous driving looks like: resilient, adaptable, and ready for anything.

For more information on the Wayve AI Driver, check out our website here: https://wayve.ai/

#RideTheWayve #EmbodiedAI #autonomousvehicles #ADAS

videos.title: Ride the Wayve with Jensen Huang
videos.description:
NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang went for a ride with Wayve’s CEO Alex Kendall to celebrate signing a letter of intent to evaluate a $500 million strategic investment in Wayve’s next funding round.

Learn more: https://wayve.ai/press/wayve-nvidia-announcement/
